title: Earth's Layers

sources.label: USGS: The Interior of the Earth
NASA Space Place: What Is an Earthquake?

review_notes: This is a simplified Year 5 model of Earth's layers.
Layer thickness and shape are adjusted for classroom visibility.
Diagram visuals are deterministic SVG, not copied from the TeacherGPT reference PDFs.

slides.title: Earth's Layers
slides.subtitle: A diagram-led lesson about the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core.

slides.title: Understand Earth's main layers and what each layer is like

cards.title: Name
cards.text: Identify the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core.

cards.title: Describe
cards.text: Use simple properties such as solid, liquid, hot, and rocky.

cards.title: Connect
cards.text: Explain why a diagram is a model, not Earth at true scale.

slides.callout: Learning intention: use a cross-section model to describe Earth's internal layers.

slides.title: Earth has layers inside it
slides.lead: Scientists use evidence and models to describe Earth's inside because we cannot travel all the way to the centre.

bullets.title: Crust
bullets.text: The thin outer rock layer where we live.

bullets.title: Mantle
bullets.text: A very thick layer of hot rock below the crust.

bullets.title: Core
bullets.text: The central part of Earth, split into outer and inner core.

slides.source_note: Source-grounded summary from USGS and NASA education pages.

slides.title: Earth cross-section
slides.lead: Use the labels to connect each vocabulary word with its position inside Earth.
slides.source_note: Deterministic SVG model; not to scale.

slides.title: From the outside toward the centre

steps.title: Start at the crust
steps.text: The crust is the thin outside layer of solid rock.

steps.title: Move through the mantle
steps.text: The mantle is much thicker and made of very hot rock.

steps.title: Reach the core
steps.text: The outer core surrounds the solid inner core near Earth's centre.

items.title: Crust
items.text: The thin outer layer of Earth.

items.title: Mantle
items.text: A thick layer of hot rock below the crust.

items.title: Outer core
items.text: A very hot liquid layer around the inner core.

items.title: Inner core
items.text: A very hot solid centre of Earth.

items.title: Model
items.text: A simplified representation that helps us understand something.

items.title: Layer
items.text: One part arranged above or below another part.

slides.title: What the layers help us explain

pairs.effect: Earth's surface is only a small part of the whole planet.
Heat inside Earth helps drive slow movement over long times.
The core is part of how scientists explain Earth's deep interior.

slides.source_note: Keep explanations age-appropriate; this is a Year 5 model.

slides.title: The diagram helps, but it is simplified
slides.lead: A classroom diagram makes hidden layers visible. It does not show true thickness, temperature, or movement exactly.

cards.title: Useful
cards.text: It shows the order of the layers clearly.

cards.title: Limited
cards.text: It changes scale and shape so students can see the parts.

slides.title: Read the diagram like a scientist

steps.label: Locate
steps.title: Find each layer
steps.text: Point to the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core.

steps.label: Compare
steps.title: Use two properties
steps.text: Describe two layers using words such as thin, thick, solid, liquid, hot, or rocky.

steps.label: Explain
steps.title: Spot the model limits
steps.text: Explain one way the diagram is simplified.

questions.q: Which layer is the thin outside layer?
Which layer sits below the crust?
Why is this diagram called a model?
questions.a: It simplifies Earth so we can understand the layers.

slides.title: Show your understanding
prompts: One Earth layer I can identify is...
One way this diagram is simplified is...
slides.challenge: Challenge: explain why scientists use models for things we cannot directly see.

slides.title: Sources used
slides.note: Visual policy: deterministic SVG diagram; no copied TeacherGPT PDF artwork.
